message:
refactor(wal,async_api): migrate worker/ticker threads to asupersync runtime

Both the WAL epoch ticker and the async Connection worker were using
std::thread + std::sync::mpsc as their concurrency primitives.  This
commit moves both over to asupersync's Runtime/BlockingTaskHandle
machinery so they participate in structured cancellation via Cx and
can be hosted on any asupersync runtime (real, test, or single-
threaded).

crates/fsqlite-wal/src/parallel_wal.rs

- ParallelWalCoordinator now stores a ticker_cx: Mutex<Option<Cx>>
  child cancellation scope and (on non-wasm) a ticker_handle:
  Mutex<Option<BlockingTaskHandle>> produced by
  runtime.spawn_blocking().  The previous
  Mutex<Option<std::thread::JoinHandle<()>>> is removed.
- start()/start_with_fsync() are replaced by
  start_on_runtime(runtime, parent_cx) and
  start_on_runtime_with_fsync(runtime, parent_cx, fsync_policy).
  Callers now hand in an explicit RuntimeHandle and parent Cx,
  which makes the coordinator's lifetime and cancellation
  semantics explicit instead of tied to a global pool.
- Start path:
    1. If already running, short-circuit with
       "coordinator already running".
    2. Drain any stale ticker_cx / ticker_handle left over from a
       previous incomplete stop() (cancel + wait).
    3. CAS self.running from false→true.  If that fails, another
       caller raced in; return the same "already running" error.
    4. parent_cx.create_child() → ticker_cx; clone for the spawn.
    5. runtime.spawn_blocking(move || epoch_ticker_loop(..., loop_cx))
       returns Option<BlockingTaskHandle>.  On None (the runtime
       has no blocking pool), roll the running flag back to false
       and return a descriptive error.
- epoch_ticker_loop now takes an extra loop_cx: Cx and checks
  loop_cx.is_cancelled() in its idle loop so a Cx::cancel() on
  the ticker scope tears the loop down cleanly instead of having
  to wait for the running flag to flip.
- stop()/remove_parallel_wal_coordinator() now cancel the
  ticker_cx and wait on the BlockingTaskHandle via handle.wait()
  instead of thread.join().
- On target_arch = "wasm32" the ticker handle and spawn path are
  feature-gated out — WASM builds stay on the synchronous
  flush path they already used.

crates/fsqlite/src/async_api.rs  (~480 line net rewrite of worker)

- Module docs updated: "dedicated worker thread" → "dedicated
  worker task" and "oneshot channels" → "response channels".
- Switch to asupersync::channel::oneshot, asupersync::cx::Cx as
  NativeCx, asupersync::runtime::{BlockingTaskHandle, Runtime,
  RuntimeBuilder, RuntimeHandle} for the worker plumbing.
- Remove the hand-rolled OneshotFuture<T> polling future (which
  used std::sync::mpsc::Receiver and a background waker thread
  per outstanding call).  Every async method now returns a
  NativeCx-backed oneshot receiver directly.
- New const WORKER_POLL_INTERVAL = 10ms for the worker's
  internal parking cadence.
- New helpers worker_open_err() / worker_dead_err() that produce
  the canonical "worker task terminated during open" /
  "worker task terminated unexpectedly" FrankenError::Internal
  messages so error text is consistent across every call site.
- AsyncConnection::open_on_runtime(runtime, cx, path) spawns
  a BlockingTask on the provided runtime, hands it a child Cx,
  and waits on a one-shot OpenResponse.  If the task dies
  before answering, we propagate worker_open_err().
- Every async query/exec/commit method now:
    1. cx.checkpoint() to pick up outbound cancellation.
    2. Sends a Command on the worker mpsc.
    3. Awaits the response via asupersync::oneshot::Receiver.
    4. Maps "worker gone" into worker_dead_err().
- Shutdown posts Command::Shutdown, waits on the worker
  BlockingTaskHandle, and cancels the child Cx so any still-
  pending commands see the cancellation on their next
  checkpoint().

crates/fsqlite/Cargo.toml
- Add asupersync = { workspace = true, optional = true } to
  [dependencies].
- The existing `async-api` feature now pulls in
  `dep:asupersync` alongside the previous
  `fsqlite-types/native` dependency, so turning the feature on
  is the single knob for enabling the asupersync-backed async
  path.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -8,6 +8,7 @@ repository.workspace = true
 rust-version.workspace = true
 
 [dependencies]
+asupersync = { workspace = true, optional = true }
 fsqlite-types = { workspace = true }
 fsqlite-error = { workspace = true }
 fsqlite-core = { version = "0.1.2", path = "../fsqlite-core", default-features = false }
@@ -33,7 +34,7 @@ rtree = ["dep:fsqlite-ext-rtree"]
 session = ["dep:fsqlite-ext-session"]
 icu = ["dep:fsqlite-ext-icu"]
 misc = ["dep:fsqlite-ext-misc"]
-async-api = ["fsqlite-types/native"]
+async-api = ["dep:asupersync", "fsqlite-types/native"]
 raptorq = []
 mvcc = []
